title: B.B.A (Hons. with Research) Aviation Management *
content: *4-year Undergraduate Degree (Honours with Research) Course Category | Credits | Common Core | 54 | Specialisation Core | 18 | Specialisation Elective | 18 | VAD | 8 | AEC | 12 | Community Service Project | 2 | Internship | 5 | Industrial Research Project | 3 | Total | 120 | 4-year UG Degree with Honours (Source: - UGC Curriculum & Credit Framework for Undergraduate Programmes) XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 01 Apply core management theories and foundational business knowledge to analyse diverse business challenges. 02 Utilise appropriate digital tools and analytical techniques to analyse business problems and inform decision-making processes. 03 Propose sustainable and ethical solutions to complex business problems, considering diverse local and global contexts. 04 Communicate effectively and collaborate seamlessly with diverse stakeholders in professional and team environments. 05 Demonstrate ethical leadership and social responsibility, integrating principles of sustainability for inclusive national development. 06 Evaluate emerging trends and technological advancements to identify entrepreneurial opportunities and drive organisational growth. 07 Engage in continuous professional development and self-directed learning to remain current in the evolving global business landscape. 01 Excel as competent operational professionals within airlines, airports, and allied aviation services, adeptly applying foundational management knowledge to ensure efficient and safe daily functions. 02 Contribute to a secure and ethically compliant aviation environment by understanding industry regulations and implementing responsible business practices in roles focused on safety, quality, or ground operations. 03 Adapt to the evolving aviation industry landscape, leveraging continuous learning and an innovative mindset to advance into supervisory or specialized analytical positions within the sector. 01 Analyze core aviation industry operations, including airline management, airport functions, and regulatory compliance, to propose efficient and safe solutions. 02 Apply aviation-specific business principles and management techniques to address challenges in areas such as aviation finance, marketing, and human resource management within the sector. 01 Foundational Knowledge: Apply foundational knowledge and understanding of core business disciplines to comprehend and analyse business situations. 02 Disciplinary Knowledge: Apply discipline-specific knowledge to understand business and operational challenges. 03 Digital Literacy & Modern Tool Usage: Select and apply appropriate information technology tools, digital platforms, and analytical software to enhance business operations, analysis, and decision-making. 04 Critical Thinking & Problem Solving: Identify, analyse, and solve business problems using logical reasoning, analytical skills, and evidence-based approaches to make informed decisions. 05 Effective Communication: Communicate business ideas, analyses, and solutions clearly, concisely, and persuasively, both orally and in writing, to diverse audiences. 06 Teamwork & Collaboration: Function effectively as an individual and as a member or leader in diverse teams, demonstrating interpersonal skills, respect for others, and the ability to collaborate towards common goals. 07 Ethical & Social Responsibility: Understand and commit to professional ethics, societal values, and sustainable business practices, demonstrating awareness of the impact of business decisions on stakeholders and the environment. 08 Lifelong Learning: Recognize the need for continuous learning and possess the ability to engage in independent, self-directed learning to adapt to the evolving business environment and enhance professional competence.
